description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story agricultural building with a broad, gently sloped gable roof clad in red shingles. The front facade features a large open entrance supported by wooden posts, with transparent panels or windows running along the lower half, allowing visibility and light into the interior. The side and rear walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, with several rectangular windows providing additional natural light and ventilation. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground, supported by concrete footings at each corner and along the front. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability, with a warm, natural wood finish that blends into rural or peri-urban environments. The roof overhangs provide shelter from rain and sun, while the open front and multiple windows ensure adequate airflow for livestock.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a red gable roof, open front entrance, and multiple side windows, supported by wooden posts and concrete footings.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open entrance with wooden posts and transparent lower panels, providing a clear look into the interior.
description_view_1: The left side view displays two rectangular windows set in horizontal wooden planks, with the roof overhanging the side.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals three rectangular windows evenly spaced along the wooden wall, with no visible entrance.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the wooden siding and roof overhang, but without visible windows.
attribute_door_style: open front entrance with wooden posts, no visible hinged or sliding doors
attribute_window_presence: five rectangular windows; two on the left side, three on the rear wall
